heading: Determination
label: 2106 Determination
text: The Supreme Court or any other court of appellate jurisdiction may affirm, modify, vacate, set aside or reverse any judgment, decree, or order of a court lawfully brought before it for review, and may remand the cause and direct the entry of such appropriate judgment, decree, or order, or require such further proceedings to be had as may be just under the circumstances.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 28, U.S.C., 1940 ed., §§ 344, 876, 877 (R.S. § 701; , §§ 10, 11, ; , §§ 231, 236, 237, 291, , 1167; , ; , ; , ; , ; , ).Mar. 3, 1891, ch. 51726 Stat. 829Mar. 3, 1911, ch. 23136 Stat. 1156Dec. 23, 1914, ch. 238 Stat. 790Sept. 16, 1916, ch. 448, § 239 Stat. 726Feb. 17, 1922, ch. 5442 Stat. 366Feb. 13, 1925, ch. 229, § 143 Stat. 937Jan. 31, 1928, ch. 14, § 145 Stat. 54Section consolidates part of , U.S.C., 1940 ed., with sections 876 and 877 of said title. Other provisions of said section 344 are incorporated in sections 1257 and 2103 of this title.section 344 of title 28Words “or a court of appeals” were inserted after “Supreme Court” upon authority of ., C.C.A. 1915, 226 F. 653, affirmed 37 S.Ct. 614, 244 U.S. 376, 61 L.Ed. 1206, wherein it was held that this section also applied to the courts of appeals in view of section 11 of the Circuit Court of Appeals , .United States v. Illinois Surety CoAct of Mar. 3, 1891, ch. 51728 Stat. 829The revised section will cover instances where the Supreme Court remands a case to the highest court of a State and to the United States Tax Court. It will also cover a remand of a case to the Court of Claims or the Court of Customs and Patent Appeals. For authority to remand a case to The Tax Court, see , 1944, 64 S.Ct. 722, 321 U.S. 560, 88 L.Ed. 927.Equitable Life Assurance Society of U.S. v. Commissioner of Internal RevenueRevised section will also permit a remand by the Supreme Court to a court of appeals inasmuch as such latter court then would be a lower court. The revised section is in conformity with numerous holdings of the Supreme Court to the effect that such a remand may be made. See especially, , 1929, 49 S.Ct. 484, 279 U.S. 792, 73 L.Ed. 960; , 1928, 48 S.Ct. 358, 276 U.S. 386, 72 L.Ed. 620 and ., 1928, 48 S.Ct. 440, 277 U.S. 226, 72 L.Ed. 860.Maryland Casualty Co. v. United StatesKrauss Bros. Co. v. MellonBuzyuski v. Luckenbach S. S. CoThe last sentence of , U.S.C., 1940 ed., providing that the Supreme Court should not issue execution but should send a special mandate to the inferior court to award execution, was omitted. See rule 34 of the revised rules of the Supreme Court relating to Mandates, and authorizing the Supreme Court to issue all writs necessary in aid of its jurisdiction.section 876 of title 28section 1651 of this titleChanges were made in phraseology.
